feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
feed.subtitle: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: E-Invoicing for self billing in France E-Invoice
entry.content: We are implementing SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) for France E‑Invoicing. As part of the mandate, we are required to use self‑invoicing; however, SAP currently does not provide a standard solution for this scenario. According to French compliance requirements, self‑invoices must be generated by us through MRRL and subsequently transmitted to the approved Plateformes de Dématérialisation Partenaires (PAs). We kindly request your guidance and a proposed solution to address this gap, as this is critical for ensuring compliance with the French e‑invoicing law. Thank you in advance for your support and prompt attention to this matter.

entry.title: SAP DRC strategy: ViDA readiness and global e-invoicing and e-reporting rollout
entry.content:
Our company runs primarily on US GAAP with limited local statutory ledger setup (no country version available) in many countries. Does that create gaps for DRC?

For example, missing local tax codes, document types or master data that the country reports require?  

How does a DRC implementation actually unfold in a country like France, E2E?

entry.title: Why is report DE_AUDIT_EUBP (DRC) only valid from 1 January 2024?
entry.content:
Hi,

my customer needs to provide the legal report DE_AUDIT_EUBP for the fiscal year 2022. But when trying to set up the actitivites, I can only choose "Valid From" 2024 and it is not possible to change. 

Is there any way to use the report for earlier fiscal years?  If not, what's the alternative?

Thank you and best regards

Rado

entry.title: Question on GTI License Requirements for China Golden Tax Integration Scenarios
entry.content:
Hi SAP colleagues,
I would like to get your advice on the license requirements related to SAP Golden Tax Interface (GTI) in China localization.
We are currently evaluating different integration approaches between SAP and the Chinese Golden Tax System, and I would like to confirm whether my understanding of the licensing implications is correct.
■ Background
We assume that SAP SD billing data is transferred to the Golden Tax Interface (GTI), where data validation, splitting, and consolidation are performed before invoice issuance.
■ Integration Scenarios
1. Manual Integration
SAP outputs billing data in the required format of the Golden Tax System.
The data is then manually processed and uploaded into the Golden Tax System to issue invoices.
2. Integration via Third-Party System
SAP sends data in the format required by a third-party vendor, and the third-party system handles the integration with the Golden Tax System.
3. Direct Integration
SAP directly sends data to the Golden Tax System via APIs/interfaces for invoice issuance.
■ Questions
1. For Scenario 1 (manual integration): If we use standard GTI functions in SAP (such as data extraction, validation, splitting, or consolidation), would a GTI-related license still be required?
2. For Scenarios 2 and 3: Is it correct to understand that a GTI (or related) license is required whenever SAP is involved in the integration process?
3. In an S/4HANA environment:
When using solutions such as
- SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC)
- China Tax e-Invoice Management how do these relate to GTI licensing?
Are additional licenses required?
Any insights or experience sharing would be greatly appreciated.
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP DRC - Electronic Invoicing for Italy : ItalyReceiveNotification without SNI
entry.content:
Hello 

I am currently implementing SAP DRC - Electronic Invoicing for Italy.
We successfully managed to send documents to SdI, but unfortunately we are facing an issue with the ItalyReceiveNotification process. When SdI calls this service, they receive an HTTP 500 error. This could be related to the fact that SdI does not support SNI.

I found a forum discussion that refers to this issue: https://forum.italia.it/t/problema-test-interoperabilita/45870/7 
But I have not been able to find a concrete solution. I would like to understand whether SAP can resolve this issue on its side, or whether it must be addressed by our network team, for example by implementing a reverse proxy.

entry.title: E-Reporting Vs E-Invoicing for External Billing Documents
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to establish a link between E-Invoicing versus E-Reporting for Invoices which are coming to SAP DRC from non-SAP system. Non-SAP systems feeds the invoice data to SAP DRC in UBL 2.1 format and then SAP DRC creates its e-Document .

In the BP we maintain country key=FR and FR2 (SIREN) with taxstatus=PRA hence making the customer as established entity in France. so it qualifies for the Domestic B2B transaction and hence the E-Invoicing.

In some customers we maintain country key=FR and FR2 (SIREN) with taxstatus=NOR hence making the customer as Non-established entity in France. so it qualifies for the Domestic B2C transaction and hence E-Reporting.

For SAP native invoices i-e Billing document generated via SD or FI postings, above BP logic is working fine and it is routing the invoice data correctly to E-Invoicing or E-Reporting folder.

For the invoices coming from non-SAP system as UBL invoice, necessary business transformation is happening in the middleware and then it is replicated to SAP DRC and SAP DRC is creating e-Invoice for every case disregarding the FR2 TAXSTATUS=PRA OR NOR.  I am not sure why SAP standard logic does not work  for these invoices coming from external systems. Has anyone encountered this issue ? Will SAP standard logic not work for non-SAP invoices coming in UBL 2.1 format and we will have to do this transformation in middleware ?

Any experiences will be appreciated.

Thanks

Atif

entry.title: the eDocument processing fails with When submitting customer invoice from EDOC_COCKPIT
entry.content:
Hallo,

When submitting a customer invoice from transaction EDOC_COCKPIT, the eDocument processing fails with error:

DCP-GOR-ERR-GEN

The invoice was successfully created and posted before submission. During the submission process, the eDocument status changes to error and cannot be processed further. The error is shown as follows: 

Please help analyze the root cause of error DCP-GOR-ERR-GEN and advise on the required corrective actions. Thanks! 

Best,

Jingjie

entry.title: Einvoicing Poland KSEF issue with chain transaction with shipping point = DE
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts,

we have an issue with SAP DRC for Poland / KSEF. We have implemented e-invoicing for our Polish location.

We are currently experiencing an issue with invoices that are not being registered for KSeF and I would like to know if any of you have the same issue?

The issue comes up, when an order is placed within the Polish company code, but production and shipping take place in Germany (shipping point = Germany), while the invoice is issued from Poland. Tax settings are correct, and the invoice destination country in the billing invoice is also set to Poland.

According to our Polish accounting department, this case must be reported to KSeF because it constitutes a chain transaction due to the internal billing involved. However, SAP does not recognize it as KSeF-relevant—since the shipping point is not in Poland—so it does not appear in the eDocument Cockpit. There is a sap note that suggests to change the shipping point, but from our point of view this is not the correct way to handle it, because it will mess up reporting and intrastat.

We've opened an SAP incident but so far got no help. Has anyone else encountered this issue, and if so, how did you resolve it?

Thanks in advance

#shippingpoint #ksef #chain transaction

entry.title: KSA VAT Return Report issue with Exempt Purchases
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have created KSR VAT Return report configuration everything is working fine but only exempt purchases.

We have two tax codes which we are using as an example 'V0 & V1' specifically for Exempt Purchases. But the value is flowing from the document which is of V2 tax code (reversal entry).

How to check if such incorrect values populate into the other column and how to rectify it.

Thanks.

Regards,

Vishnu

entry.title: SAP DRC Poland: JPK_KR_PD File Splitting Limitations for Manual Downloads (>100MB)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We are currently implementing the mandatory SAF-T requirement for Poland (JPK_KR_PD - Accounting Books) using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) on S/4HANA.

Due to our client's high transactional volume, the generated ledger data easily exceeds the 100MB limit mandated by the Polish Ministry of Finance (Ministerstwo Finansów) for package submissions.

While automated transmission through the SAP DRC Cloud handles payload splitting, we have encountered a major bottleneck in the manual processing/local download path. When extracting the file manually through the Run Compliance Reports app to handle it outside the direct cloud channel, SAP DRC generates a single, massive XML file and does not apply any native splitting logic to break it down into sub-100MB chunks.

We would appreciate the community's insights on the following:

Product Architecture: Why does the standard manual solution lack native XML file-splitting parameters out-of-the-box for JPK_KR_PD? Is this a known limitation of the DRC manual download framework, or is there a specific SAP Note/BAdI configuration we are missing to activate splitting for local files?

Workarounds & Solutions: How are your projects solving this issue when direct API submission isn't an option or during hypercare/manual testing phases?

  • Are you utilizing custom ABAP file-splitter utilities post-generation?
  •  Have you developed custom logic within the DRC framework to partition the data by document ranges/dates prior to XML serialization?
  • Are you relying on external middleware/third-party tools to slice the monolithic XML?

Any advice, structural workarounds, or reference SAP Notes from your ongoing Poland compliance rollouts would be incredibly valuable.

Thank you,
Felipe

entry.title: Submitting XML via Mail with SAP DRC and SAP ERP
entry.content:
Hello

I would like to understand how the email dispatch of electronic outgoing invoices for Germany works with SAP DRC and SAP ERP.

The illustration on the SAP help page indicates that the DRC Cloud Edition is required:

Submitting Customer Invoices Using E-Mail (XML) | SAP Help Portal

Is the DRC Cloud Edition really necessary to send the XML file created in SAP AIF to a customer by email?

Many thanks.

Maurice

entry.title: SAP DRC France on SAP ERP ECC6 EHP6 – Inconsistent eDocument creation in EDOC_COCKPIT
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are currently implementing SAP DRC France on SAP ERP ECC6 EHP6 for the upcoming France e-invoicing / e-reporting mandate.

All recommended SAP Notes have been implemented, and the customizing described in SAP Help Portal has been followed for:

  • Electronic Invoicing and Reporting via Approved Platform
  • Electronic Invoicing via Peppol

After running several tests, we are still facing inconsistent behavior in EDOC_COCKPIT.

Current behavior observed:

  • For FI customer invoices, eDocuments are created for French customers with or without SIREN, but not for foreign customers.
  • For SD billing documents, eDocuments are created for French customers without SIREN and for foreign customers, but not for French customers with SIREN.
  • SD credit memos are displayed in the cockpit, but with an incorrect document/process description.
  • FI B2B International invoices and credit memos are not displayed in the cockpit.
  • For one FI B2B International invoice, the document is displayed only when the currency is different from EUR, but with an incorrect description.
  • Full and partial incoming payments posted with F-28 do not generate the expected payment reporting in the cockpit.

Have you encountered similar behavior on SAP ERP / ECC for SAP DRC France?

Do you know if:

  • only the Electronic Invoicing and Reporting via Approved Platform customizing should be maintained for the France B2B mandate;
  • maintaining both customizing nodes can impact eDocument/process determination;
  • there are additional SAP Notes, BC Sets, BAdIs or configuration points to check for FI vs SD determination;
  • specific prerequisites are required for payment reporting from F-28?

Any feedback or return of experience would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Portugal Periodic VAT Return and Annexes S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am currently configuring the Portugal Periodic VAT Return and respective annexes in the Fiori environment.

At the moment, our export and import declaration numbers are stored in the following fields:

  • BKPF-XREF1_HD (Export)
  • BKPF-XREF2_HD (Import)

However, in the Fiori application, only the fields XBLNR (Reference) and BKTXT (Document Header Text) are available and displayed.

Currently, our process is executed via transaction PTFO10 to generate the annexes, where we are able to populate:

  • BKPF-XREF1_HD for customers
  • BKPF-XREF2_HD for vendors

Given this, I would like to ask:
Is it possible to include the fields BKPF-XREF1_HD and BKPF-XREF2_HD in the Fiori application for the VAT return/annexes?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Deivid

entry.title: SAP DRC RFC oAuth2 code 404
entry.content:
Hello the community,

First time here and I'm asking to myself if someone has the same case than me.

I did all the configuration for SAP DRC relevant for our SAP ECC6 EHP8 (SP24) system.

The connection from Cloud Connector to the backend is OK and the ressource is available. The connection from BTP to the backend through the dedicated destination is OK when I test it. My issue is located on the created RFC type G I had to create with the oAuth2 configuration. I did all the chapter "Managing RFC Connections for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition" of the documentation : https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/managing-rfc-connections-for-sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition?locale=en-US

I first met two scenarios.

  • When I first tested the connetion, I met an error Create failed: plugin not active

So I checked note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003412080/E without success.

  • Then, I figured out that I didn't have both certificate G3/G5 in my STRUST. So I put them and I've retested the connection. I went from Create failed: plugin not active to error code 404 and not 401 as mentioned in the documentation

I really don't know where I failed. In SE80, all is done for the dedicated package.

In OA2C_CONFIG, I mentioned the clientID, clientSecret and the related URLs.

Authorization Endpoint : <enterprise>-drc-cloud-dev.authentication.eu02.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorization

Token Endpoint : <enterprise>-drc-cloud-dev.authentication.eu02.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token

And the RFC configuration as been done as described...

Type : G

Host : drcc-test.c-257d320.kyma.ondemand.com

Path Prefix : /

Port : 443

Logon with User : Do not use a user

=> OAuth Settings => OA2C profile selected

Security Options, SSL Active

=> SSL Certificate ANONYM SSL Client, in which the ISRG Root X1 certificate has been set.

All the relevant oAuth2 SICF services seem to be activated.

The report OA2C_GENERIC_ACCESS return seems to be OK.

I'm still checking with SAP through a dedicated case but I don't know if I'm well understanded.

Thank you for your time and thank you if someone can help.

Have a nice day.

Regards.

Axel .

entry.title: What are the mandatory value mapping for the eDocument Invoice for Germany in Public Cloud?
entry.content: What are the mandatory value mapping for the eDocument Invoice for Germany in the SAP S4/HANA Public Cloud? Is there an SAP Note that lists all maintained value mapping. I can't find an official framework. Mapping Fields to the XML File What other fields need to be filled out to comply with German accounting regulations? CUST_DEBIT_BANK_ACC - SALES_ORG_PART_FUNC - TAX_EXEMPT_CODE - INVOICE_TYPE_CODE_FI - INVOICE_TYPE_CODE_SD - PAYMENT_MEANS_CODE - TAX_CATEGORY - UNIT_OF_MEASURE_CODE - TOT_VALUE_TAX_TAXCD - INVOICE_TYPE_CODE_CI - SETTINGS - CONDITION_RELEVANCE - CONDITION_RELEVANCE - CONDITION_RELEVANCE

entry.title: DRC - alert when invoice retrieval job fails
entry.content:
Hello,

we use DRC for E-Invoices Through KSeF 2.0 (Poland).

We have a hourly job that retrieves incoming invoices. There's been occurrences where the job has failed and didn't retrieve a few invoices. When this happens, one or more entries appear in the "Resolve errors" section of the DRC: from there I can reprocess the errors.

What I would like to know is if there's a way to receive an alert when this happens, or if I need to manually check the "Resolve errors".

Thanks, kind regards.

entry.title: DRC Germany- Unable to receive supplier email with xml in Manage Electronic documents app
entry.content:
Hello friends,

We are implementing SAP DRC for a S4HANA 2025 PCE system for Germany. We are using OpenText VIM as an automation solution. It seems we have followed all the steps, but still when we send test email with an xml invoice attachment, it does not land up in the Manage Electronic documents app.

May be we are missing something. Need guidance Please!

Best regards,

Vishal S Thakur

entry.title: SAP DRC EDI / IDOC Support
entry.content:
Does anyone have experience with the established EDI process in relation to electronic invoicing in Germany?

Many customers exchange outgoing invoices via EDI. This includes, amongst other things, the intercompany process in SAP, where an outgoing invoice can be automatically posted as an incoming invoice in the requesting company code via an IDoc.

According to the law, it is stated that this electronic procedure may continue to be used as long as the format is interoperable with EN 16931 and all the necessary information can be extracted. But what exactly does that mean? How should interoperability be achieved? Are there already functions for this in SAP DRC?

Many thanks
Best regards
Maurice

entry.title: S4/H Public Cloud - Run statutory reports
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Good Day!

I am using the Run Statutory Reports app to generate the GSTR-3B report. In the configuration, I have maintained the Reporting Category ID – IN_GSTR3B_DCL under Set Up Your Statutory Reporting (SSCUI 106604).

I have a requirement to include the Quantity and Unit of Measure (UOM) columns in the report output.

While going through SAP blogs, I came across information suggesting that this can be achieved by customising the report using Define Statutory Reports. However, I am unsure how to proceed with this configuration.

Could anyone please guide me on the steps involved, or let me know if this requirement is supported in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards,

Sam Ebinazer

entry.title: Automate PDF upload to edcoument for Germany for ZUGFeRD
entry.content:
When I am going to link S4HANA On prem E-invoice Germany Implementation, I can see that SAP is writing:

Requirements for Custom Development to Upload PDF to eDocuments | SAP Help Portal

You must integrate your own custom development to upload PDFs to electronic document processing for Germany.

Your development must call the UPLOAD_FILE_TO_EDOC method of the CL_EDOC_API_FILE class. For testing purposes, you can use the Upload File to eDocument (Example Report) (EDOC_UPLOAD_FILE_EXAMPLE) to call the class method. For more information on this class method, refer to the documentation of this object directly in the system.
I can create a program and make a batch job to add the PDF to Edocument after validation of XML but is there any place or enhancement point that I could place the code and it will be a automated process. Please suggest if you have done something like this or anything, am I missing somthing?
